9MR5-X64: Edmund Bridges was born in 1637 in Lynn, Massachusetts Bay Colony, and made his living as a blacksmith. He died in 1682 in Salem, where his estate entered probate that November. He was buried without a headstone.
24L6-971: John Orrick lived his entire life in Anne Arundel County, Maryland, where he was born in 1685 and died in 1749 in the days of British Colonial America.
L6F7-GFF: Benjamin Weeks Sr. was born in 1685 in Falmouth in the old Plymouth Colony and eventually settled far to the south in White Oak Township, Carteret County, North Carolina. He drew up his will there on 9 November 1744 and died the same year; the document was witnessed by Jehoshaphat Hollands, Francis Burns, and Thomas Person, and was probated in June 1745.
LZ1Y-1DH: Matthew Barnes Sr. was born in 1670 in Port Tobacco, Charles County, Maryland, and lived there until his death in 1746, when his estate passed through probate that February.
LDMY-5LD: John Lamont Young was born in Scotland in 1671 and bore the title of Sir. He married in Dublin in 1694 and made his home for a time in Antrim, Ireland, before arriving in New York in 1710. He lived to a remarkable old age, dying in Augusta County, Virginia, in 1783.
GPWS-R74: James de Saix Crocketagne was born in 1674 at Bantry Bay in County Cork, Ireland, and died there in 1767. He is remembered with a Find A Grave memorial.
LTRK-JDX: John Chaffin was born in 1716 in Christchurch, Middlesex County, Virginia, and lived in the colony all his life. He resided in Southam Parish in Cumberland County, Virginia, where he died in 1773.
M85Z-N28: Philip Friedrich Cullmann was born in 1715 in Hornbach in the Palatinate region of present-day Germany and died in 1791 in Baumholder, Birkenfeld.
LX39-ZC9: William McGehee was born in 1677 in St. Peter's Parish, New Kent County, Virginia, and was a Quaker, an affiliation recorded for him in both 1702 and 1734. He held land, including a thousand-acre patent, and wrote his will in 1727 at St. John's Parish in King William County. He died in Caroline County, Virginia, in 1748.
LBSS-P4B: John Lamont McGorrie was the Baron of Knockdow and the fourteenth Chief of Clan Lamont, born in 1619 at Knockdow in Argyll, Scotland. He resided at Rhu and later at Castle Toward. His life ended violently in 1646, when he was hanged at Dunoon in the infamous Dunoon Massacre.
KN6H-19Y: Ann West Owsley was born in 1744 in Fairfax Parish, Virginia, and later moved south to Laurens County, South Carolina, where she was recorded living in 1800 and 1810. She died there in 1823.
L7J6-S7W: Ulrich Stähle was born in 1601 in Württemberg, Germany, and worked as a Bürger und Anwalt, a citizen and lay advocate. His life was marked by frequent moves across the Göppingen region and beyond, migrating through Donzdorf, Hohenstaufen, Eschenbach, and Zell over several decades. He died in 1689 in Börtlingen.
LCWK-21J: Nicholas Outhouse was born in 1726 in Queens, New York, and was baptized that September. A United Empire Loyalist, he left New York and made his way to Canada, residing for a time at Sissiboo Falls in Nova Scotia before arriving in 1797. He died in 1804 in Charlotteville, Norfolk County, Ontario.
LX6S-9TB: George Proctor was born in 1624 in Jamestown, Virginia, and his passage from England to Surry County was paid by John Rawlins. He lived in Surry County, Virginia, where he died in 1678, leaving behind estate and land records that ran into the early 1700s.
LRRR-C6G: Robert Lamont Young was born in 1711 in County Antrim, Ireland, and emigrated with his family to America, arriving in 1740. He and his wife Agnes Crockett Young settled in the Back Creek area of Augusta County, Virginia, where he died in 1755.
LRSV-ZGT: Mary Williams was born in 1633 in the Plymouth Colony and made her home in Rhode Island. She died in 1681 in Providence.
GP19-1NW: Alexander Southerland was born in Scotland and immigrated to America around 1654, settling in the New Kent County area of colonial Virginia, where he later died. Little else is documented about his life with certainty.
LCPP-Y5C: Newdigate Poyntz was born in 1608 in Reigate, Surrey, England, and resided for a time in Oxford. He died in 1643 in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire.
LYKW-HCR: Margaret Hanford was born in 1619 in Devon, England. In the spring of 1635 she sailed from London aboard the Planter under Master Nicholas Trerice, arriving in Boston on 7 June. She settled in the Plymouth Colony and died in 1649 in Barnstable.
LCXQ-RD6: John Roberts was born in 1655 in Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony, and died in 1714 in Gloucester, Essex County, in the same colony.
LCCZ-YTW: Deliverance Rice was born in 1681 in Sudbury, Middlesex County, Massachusetts Bay Colony, and died in 1723 in nearby Marlborough.
LZFW-WKG: Mary Hunt was born in 1620 in Gretton, Northamptonshire, England, and immigrated to America in 1635. She settled in Isle of Wight County, Virginia, where she lived out her life and died in 1685; her will was written in 1727 and probated in 1732.
K24Q-RHB: Jean Arlu was born in 1673 in Cannes, France, and crossed the Atlantic to the colony of Louisiana in New France. There he worked as a master builder in Mobile, where he died in 1725.
LR9P-H8K: Simon Claessen van Uythuysen was born in 1623 in Uithuizen, Groningen, in the Netherlands, and lived a seafaring life as a ship's captain, commanding vessels including The Graft, the Flower of Gelder, and De St. Jan Baptist through the 1650s. He and his wife Annekin lived at the Poor Bowery before buying land in Brooklyn near the ferry in 1662, and by 1676 he appeared on the Brooklyn tax list. He died in Brooklyn in 1684.
KND9-BM3: Maj. Robert Harris was born in Wales in 1635 and died in 1701 in Jamestown, Virginia, in the era of British Colonial America.
9MDG-WFS: Humilitie Wheaten was born in 1588 in Crediton, Devon, England, and was of the Anglican faith. Around 1635 she immigrated to the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and she lived to a great age, dying in Braintree in 1687.
LDLR-94G: Barbara Magdalena Kilchling was born in 1668 in Malsburg-Marzell in the Lörrach district of present-day Germany and was baptized that September in Müllheim, Baden. She died in 1718 in Baden-Württemberg.
L5GZ-SFC: Francis Hester was born in 1650 in Oxford, England, and arrived in New Kent County, Virginia, from England in 1704, where the quit rent rolls show him holding 300 acres. He took part in the parish processioning of land, though by late 1708 he was described as too aged to travel far, content simply to see his own boundaries walked. He died in 1720 in St. Peter's Parish, New Kent County, the church believed to be his burial place.
